title: fluffy warm spanish semolina casserole
description: a winey, pillowy, hangover-cure spanish (andalusian) dish featuring semolina, rest-meat, sugar-free-friendly ingredients
instructions: in a large skillet, cook the ground beef over medium-high heat until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent.
pour in the warmed chicken broth and red wine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
stir in the semolina and cook for 2-3 minutes, until the mixture is well combined and slightly thickened.
in a large bowl, whisk together the beaten eggs and grated cheese.
add the cooked semolina mixture to the egg mixture and stir until well combined.
transfer the mixture to a 9x13-inch baking dish and smooth the top.
dot the top of the casserole with butter and sprinkle with chopped parsley.
bake the casserole in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until set and golden brown.
remove the casserole from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
